subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Why Children Overheat Faster
description: A 60-second lesson on Why Children Overheat Faster.

hook.text: A child's body heats up 3-5 times faster than an adult's in a hot car. Their thermostat is overwhelmed.

buildup.text: Children have a higher surface-area-to-body-mass ratio, absorbing environmental heat faster.

discovery.text: Their sweat glands are underdeveloped—they produce less sweat per gland than adults.

twist.text: A car interior reaches 125°F in minutes on a 75°F day. Cracking windows does almost nothing.

climax.text: Vehicular heatstroke kills an average of 38 children per year in the U.S.—all preventable.

punchline.text: Small bodies, big heat gain, weak cooling. Never leave a child in a car.

quiz.question: Why do children overheat faster than adults?
options: Higher surface-area-to-mass ratio and underdeveloped sweat glands
